web.description: Save hours on your literature review. Use ResearchRabbit to find related papers, build citation maps, and track research trends — powered by AI.
web.markdown:
# Follow your   curiosity

Keep your literature search quick, easy,

and organized and dive down the rabbit

hole of discovery.

[Jump in](http://app.researchrabbit.ai/)

## Trusted by Researchers At:

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f2c55ef080fba491d23ae6_harvard_logo.webp)![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f2c56cf046a38f545367c2_berkeley_logo.webp)![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f2c57d7d5726dee3b7f08b_cnrs_logo.webp)![Peking University uses ResearchRabbit](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68d5c69fb155229225fc6c5d_peking_logo.webp)![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f2c592c11a0c1bd0855c21_eth_logo.webp)![Stanford uses ResearchRabbit](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68d5c6c1b250c581ee7dc0f5_stanford-logo.webp)![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f2c5a91c1ea4d9df9fd215_imperial_college_logo.webp)![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68d5c69a410c83d6dfcb66ff_tum_logo.png)![Oxford University uses ResearchRabbit](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68d5c69426ae66171e3992ea_oxford_logo.webp)![National University of Singapore NUS uses ResearchRabbit](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68d5c68bc3fab0c2f038ede7_nus_logo.webp)![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f2c5c877d91ee5e7ffba0d_mit_logo.webp)![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68d5c67a9baa3e0eaf3a2f09_ucl_logo.png)![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f2c5da2066f5bdc356f4ef_max_plank_logo.webp)![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f2c5e96824d6c71c5311cb_nih_logo.webp)![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68d5c6699461a1c0f34f7026_toronto_logo.webp)![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f2c609d00090db73c3fca5_cambridge_logo.webp)![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f2c6143b717d86cfae0f7d_lmu_logo.webp)

## Let your ideas multiply

![Explore literature with a visual map](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f8224511d07142181923e4_image%201627.webp)

### Explore the literature

Endless article lists are great… if you’re a robot.

With ResearchRabbit, exploring the literature feels more like following your curiosity than checking boxes. Start with one paper and easily expand your search by adding new authors, related works, and emerging topics. The more you explore, the more your ideas grow.

Leave the lists to the robots. You’ve got discoveries to make.

[Find papers](http://app.researchrabbit.ai/)

![Explore literature with a visual map](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f8224511d07142181923e4_image%201627.webp)

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68e4398eecf34947b5dd1fe2_Frame%203860-3.webp)![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68d5f93e47ed1452459c60fa_RR_4_Best%20of%20the%20Bunch.webp)

### Stay organized

Writer’s block is just a bunch of lonely ideas waiting to be introduced.

ResearchRabbit helps you keep your papers, notes, and thoughts connected, so no idea gets left out in the cold. The best breakthroughs can happen when your ideas finally meet.

[Write better](http://app.researchrabbit.ai/)

![Understand your topic with ResearchRabbit mapping](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f95dfed11ebcd935924c50_underestand_topic.webp)

### Understand your topic

It’s hard to study an elephant with a microscope, but that’s what we’re doing when we read one paper to understand a whole field.

Instead, use ResearchRabbit to easily connect  papers and authors to the big picture. See how topics relate to each other and evolve over time by using built-in visualizations.

You can put that microscope down (for now).

[Explore connections](http://app.researchrabbit.ai/)

![Understand your topic with ResearchRabbit mapping](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f95dfed11ebcd935924c50_underestand_topic.webp)

## Why ResearchRabbit?

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f7f4ae3ffa989f2b908d9e_1d2cf9388f507236a63998a80993d37d_Global%20Medium.webp)

### Trusted by 1,000,000+ researchers worldwide

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f7f4ae472630063cc71a02_Book_Medium.webp)

### Access over 270 million academic papers

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f7f4aea7c2bd5e5202bc0a_Lecture%20Medium.webp)

### Trusted by thousands of institutions and research groups worldwide

[Skip to main content](https://scispace.com/resources/online-tools-for-easy-literature-review/#main)

[Research Writing](https://scispace.com/resources/tag/research-writing/)

# 5 literature review tools to ace your research (+2 bonus tools)

This article talks about online tools that will help streamline the literature review process and improve your productivity.

May 29, 2023— [Sucheth](https://scispace.com/resources/author/sucheth/ "Sucheth"), [Arpit Sinha](https://scispace.com/resources/author/arpit/ "Arpit Sinha"), [Madalsa](https://scispace.com/resources/author/madalsa/ "Madalsa")

![5 literature review tools to ace your research (+2 bonus tools)](https://scispace.com/resources/content/images/size/w2400/2022/07/online-tools-for-easy-literature-review.jpg)

## 5 literature review tools to ace your research (+2 bonus tools)

Your [literature review](https://scispace.com/resources/the-ultimate-guide-to-writing-a-good-literature-review/) is the lore behind your [research paper](https://scispace.com/resources/research-paper-introduction/). It comes in two forms, [systematic and scoping](https://scispace.com/resources/evaluating-literature-review-systematic-review-vs-scoping-review), both serving the purpose of rounding up previously published works in your research area that led you to write and finish your own.

A literature review is vital as it provides the reader with a critical overview of the existing body of knowledge, your methodology, and an opportunity for research applications.

![Tips-For-Writing-A-Literature-Review](https://scispace.com/resources/content/images/2023/09/Tips-for-writing-a-literature-review.jpg)

Some steps to follow while writing your review:

- Pick an accessible topic for your paper
- Do thorough research and gather evidence surrounding your topic
- Read and take notes diligently or you can use [ChatPDF](https://scispace.com/chat-pdf?ref=scispace.com) tool for this
- Create a rough structure for your review
- Synthesis your notes and write the first draft
- Edit and proofread your literature review

To make your workload a little lighter, there are many literature review AI tools. These tools can help you find academic articles through AI and answer questions about a research paper.

## **Best literature review tools to improve research workflow**

A literature review is one of the most critical yet tedious stages in composing a research paper. Many students find it an uphill task since it requires [extensive reading and careful organization](https://scispace.com/papers/a-guide-to-writing-the-dissertation-literature-review-4v46jnyp0x?ref=scispace.com).

Using some of the best literature review tools listed here, you can make your life easier by overcoming some of the existing challenges in literature reviews. From collecting and classifying to analyzing and publishing research outputs, these tools help you with your literature review and improve your productivity without additional effort or expenses.

## **1\. SciSpace**

SciSpace is an AI for academic research that will help find research papers and answer questions about a research paper. You can discover, read, and understand research papers with [SciSpace](https://scispace.com/?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=text1&utm_campaign=literature_review_tools&&utm_id=scispaceresources) making it an excellent platform for literature review. Featuring a repository with over 270 million research papers, it comes with your AI research assistant called [Copilot](https://scispace.com/resources/introducing-copilot-ai-assistant-explains-research-papers/) that offers explanations, [summaries](https://scispace.com/resources/ai-summary-generators/), and answers as you read.

Get started now:

[SciSpace by Typeset \| Discover, Create, Publish, and Promote your research paper\\
\\
Your platform to explore and explain papers. Search for 270M+ papers, understand them in simple language, and find connected papers, authors, topics.\\
\\
![](https://typeset.io/icon.png)SciSpace by Typeset\\
\\
![](https://typeset.io/vs/meta-images/home-page.jpg)](https://typeset.io/?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=clickable&utm_campaign=literature_review_tools&utm_id=scispaceresources)

### **Find academic articles through AI**

SciSpace has a dedicated literature review tool that finds scientific articles when you search for a question. Based on semantic search, it shows all the research papers relevant for your subject. You can then gather quick insights for all the papers displayed in your search results like methodology, dataset, etc., and figure out all the papers relevant for your research.

### **Identify relevant articles faster**

Abstracts are not always enough to determine whether a paper is relevant to your research question. For starters, you can ask questions to your AI research assistant, SciSpace Copilot to explore the content and better understand the article. Additionally, use the summarize feature to quickly review the methodology and results of a paper and decide if it is worth reading in detail.

![Quickly skim through the paper and focus on the most relevant information with summarize and brainstorm questions feature on SciSpace Copilot](https://scispace.com/resources/content/images/2023/05/Summarize-your-lengthy-text-blocks-with-SciSpace-Copilot-Summarize-feature.png)Summarize Feature

### **Learn in your preferred language**

A big barrier non-native English speakers face while conducting a literature review is that a significant portion of scientific literature is published in English. But with SciSpace Copilot, you can review, interact, and learn from research papers in any language you prefer — presently, it supports 75+ languages. The AI will answer questions about a [research paper](https://scispace.com/resources/writing-conclusion-for-a-research-paper/) in your mother tongue.

![Read and understand scientific literature in over 75 languages with SciSpace Copilot](https://scispace.com/resources/content/images/2023/05/Read-and-understand-scientific-literature-in-more-than-75-languages.png)Supports 75+ languages

### **Integrates with Zotero**

Many researchers use Zotero to create a library and manage research papers. SciSpace lets you import your scientific articles directly from Zotero into your SciSpace library and use Copilot to comprehend your research papers. You can also highlight key sections, add notes to the PDF as you read, and even turn helpful explanations and answers from Copilot into notes for future review.

![Directly import papers to read from your Zotero library and analyze them on SciSpace using Copilot](https://scispace.com/resources/content/images/2023/05/import-papers-to-read-from-your-Zotero-library-for-literature-review.gif)Zotero integration

### Understand math and complex concepts quickly

Come across complex mathematical equations or difficult concepts? Simply highlight the text or select the formula or table, and [Copilot](https://scispace.com/resources/scispace-copilot-chrome-extension/) will provide an explanation or breakdown of the same in an easy-to-understand manner. You can ask follow-up questions if you need further clarification.

![Understand math and tables in research papers](https://scispace.com/resources/content/images/2023/05/Get-explanations-for-math-and-tables-in-research-papers.png)Explain math

### Discover new papers to read without leaving

Highlight phrases or sentences in your research paper to get suggestions for related papers in the field and save time on literature reviews. You can also use the 'Trace' feature to move across and discover connected papers, authors, topics, and more.

![Find related papers quickly](https://scispace.com/resources/content/images/2023/05/Make-literature-review-easier-with-related-papers.png)Find related papers quickly

_SciSpace Copilot is now available as a [Chrome extension](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/scispace-copilot/cipccbpjpemcnijhjcdjmkjhmhniiick?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=text2&utm_campaign=literature_review_tools&&utm_id=scispaceresources), allowing you to access its features directly while you browse scientific literature anywhere across the web._

[SciSpace Copilot\\
\\
Your AI research assistant for understanding scientific literature.\\
\\
![](https://ssl.gstatic.com/chrome/webstore/images/icon_144px.png)Chrome Web Store\\
\\
![](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/J8s-pfsJVsfYMdna1q7ARGc6oZ1yDPQsKtagX0DWAsJP2GowuivquZiQvuQ2jJWvcF0IIaN3_EoA9PX9oLKdUv05aw=w128-h128-e365-rj-sc0x00ffffff)](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/scispace-copilot/cipccbpjpemcnijhjcdjmkjhmhniiick?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=clickable&utm_campaign=literature_review_tools&utm_id=scispaceresources)

### Get citation-backed answers

When you're conducting a literature review, you want credible information with proper references.  Copilot ensures that every piece of information provided by SciSpace Copilot is backed by a direct reference, boosting transparency, accuracy, and trustworthiness.

Ask a question related to the paper you're delving into. Every response from Copilot comes with a clickable citation. This citation leads you straight to the section of the PDF from which the answer was extracted.

![Citation-backed-answers-by-scispace](https://scispace.com/resources/content/images/2023/09/Citation-internlinking--10sec--_1-1.gif)Citation-backed answers by SciSpace Copilot

By seamlessly integrating answers with citations, SciSpace Copilot assures you of the authenticity and relevance of the information you receive.

## **2\. Mendeley**

Mendeley Citation Manager is a free web and desktop application. It helps simplify your citation management workflow significantly. Here are some ways you can speed up your referencing game with Mendeley.

### **Generate citations and bibliographies**

Easily add references from your [Mendeley library](https://www.mendeley.com/guides/mendeley-reference-manager/06.-generating-citations-and-bibliographies?ref=scispace.com) to your Word document, change your citation style, and create a bibliography, all without leaving your document.

### Retrieve references

It allows you to [access your references](https://www.mendeley.com/guides/mendeley-reference-manager/03.-searching-and-organizing-references?ref=scispace.com) quickly. Search for a term, and it will return results by referencing the year, author, or source.

### **Add PDFs**

Add sources to your Mendeley library by dragging PDF to Mendeley Reference Manager. Mendeley will automatically remove the PDF(s) metadata and create a library entry.‌

### **Read and annotate documents**

It helps you highlight and comment across multiple PDFs while keep them all in one place using [Mendeley Notebook](https://www.mendeley.com/guides/mendeley-reference-manager/04.-reading-and-annotating-documents?ref=scispace.com). Notebook pages are not tied to a reference and let you quote from many PDFs.

## **3\. Zotero**

A big part of many literature review workflows, [Zotero](https://www.zotero.org/?ref=scispace.com) is a free, open-source tool for managing citations that works as a plug-in on your browser. It helps you gather the information you need, cite your sources, lets you attach PDFs, notes, and images to your citations, and create bibliographies.

### **Import research articles to your database**

Search for research articles on a keyword, and add relevant results to your database. Then, select the articles you are most interested in, and import them into Zotero.

### **Add bibliography in a variety of formats**

With Zotero, you don’t have to scramble for different bibliography formats. Simply use the Zotero-Word plug-in to insert in-text citations and generate a bibliography.

### **Share your research**

You can save a paper and sync it with an online library to easily share your research for group projects. Zotero can be used to create your database and decrease the time you spend formatting citations.

## **4\. Sysrev**

[Sysrev](https://sysrev.com/?ref=scispace.com) is an AI too for article review that facilitates screening, collaboration, and data extraction from academic publications, abstracts, and PDF documents using machine learning. The platform is free and supports public and [Open Access](https://scispace.com/resources/open-access-publishing-ebook/) projects only.

Some of the features of Sysrev include:

### **Group labels**

Group labels can be a powerful concept for creating database tables from documents. When exported and re-imported, each group label creates a new table. To make labels for a project, go into the **manage -> labels** section of the project.

Group labels enable project managers to pull table information from documents. It makes it easier to communicate review results for specific articles.

### **Track reviewer performance**

Sysrev's label counting tool provides filtering and visualization options for keeping track of the distribution of labels throughout the project's progress. Project managers can check their projects at any point to track progress and the reviewer's performance.

### **Tool for concordance**

The Sysrev tool for concordance allows project administrators and reviewers to perform analysis on their labels. Concordance is measured by calculating the number of times users agree on the labels they have extracted.

## **5\. Colandr**

[Colandr](https://www.colandrcommunity.com/?ref=scispace.com) is a free, open-source, internet-based analysis and screening software used as an AI for academic research. It was designed to ease collaboration across various stages of the systematic review procedure. The tool can be a little complex to use. So, here are the steps involved in working with Colandr.

### **Create a review**

The first step to using Colandr is setting up an organized review project. This is helpful to librarians who are assisting researchers with systematic reviews.

### **Planning**

The planning stage is setting the review's objectives along with research queries. Any reviewer can review the details of the planning stage. However, they can only be modified by the author for the review.

### Citation screening/import

In this phase, users can upload their results from database searches. Colandr also offers an automated deduplication system.

### **Full-text screening**

The system in Colandr will discover the combination of terms and expressions that are most useful for the reader. If an article is selected, it will be moved to the final step.

### **Data extraction/export**

Colandr data extraction is more efficient than the manual method. It creates the form fields for data extraction during the planning stage of the review procedure. Users can decide to revisit or modify the form for data extraction after completing the initial screening.

### **Bonus literature review tools**

**6\. SRDR+**

SRDR+ is a web-based tool for extracting and managing systematic review or meta-analysis data. It is open and has a searchable archive of systematic reviews and their data.

**7\. Plot Digitizer**

Plot Digitizer is an efficient tool for extracting information from graphs and images, equipped with many features that facilitate data extraction. The program comes with a free online application, which is adequate to extract data quickly.

## **Final thoughts**

Writing a literature review is not easy. It’s a time-consuming process, which can become tiring at times. The literature review tools mentioned in this blog do an excellent job of maximizing your efforts and helping you write literature reviews much more efficiently. With them, you can breathe a sigh of relief and give more time to your research.

As you dive into your literature review, don’t forget to use SciSpace [ResearchGPT](https://www.researchgpt.com/?utm_source=scispace-resources&utm_medium=blog&utm_campaign=researchgpt-landing-page-visithttps://typ.st/49yLIyi) to streamline the process. It facilitates your research and helps you explore key findings, summary, and other components of the paper easily.

## **Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)**

### 1\. What is RRL in research?

RRL stands for Review of Related Literature and sometimes interchanged with ‘Literature Review.’ RRL is a body of studies relevant to the topic being researched. These studies may be in the form of journal articles, books, reports, and other similar documents. Review of related literature is used to support an argument or theory being made by the researcher, as well as to provide information on how others have approached the same topic.


### 2\. What are few softwares and tools available for literature review?

While composing an effective literature review, you can use the following tools as assistance:

• SciSpace Discover

• Mendeley

• Zotero

• Sysrev

• Colandr

• SRDR+

### 3\. How to generate an online literature review?

The Scispace Discover tool, which offers an excellent repository of millions of peer-reviewed articles and resources, will help you generate or create a literature review easily. You may find relevant information by utilizing the filter option, checking its credibility, tracing related topics and articles, and citing in widely accepted formats with a single click.



### 4\. What does it mean to synthesize literature?

To synthesize literature is to take the main points and ideas from a number of sources and present them in a new way. The goal is to create a new piece of writing that pulls together the most important elements of all the sources you read. Make recommendations based on them, and connect them to the research.


### 5\. Should we write abstract for literature review?

Abstracts, particularly for the literature review section, are not required. However, an abstract for the research paper, on the whole, is useful for summarizing the paper and letting readers know what to expect from it. It can also be used to summarize the main points of the paper so that readers have a better understanding of the paper's content before they read it.



### 6\. How do you evaluate the quality of a literature review?

A few criteria that can assess the quality of a literature review include:

• Whether it is clear and well-written.

• Whether Information is current and up to date.

• Does it cover all of the relevant sources on the topic.

• Does it provide enough evidence to support its conclusions.

### 7\. Is literature review mandatory?

Yes. Literature review is a mandatory part of any research project. It is a critical step in the process that allows you to establish the scope of your research and provide a background for the rest of your work.


### 8\. What are the sources for a literature review?

There are 3 different sources for any literature review -

Primary sources:

• Reports

• Theses

• Conference proceedings

• Company reports

• Some government publications

Secondary sources:

• Journals

• Books

• Newspapers

• Articles by professional associations

Tertiary sources:

• Indexes

• Databases

• Catalogues

• Encyclopaedias

• Dictionaries

• Bibliographies

• Citation indexes

• Statistical data from government websites

### 9\. What is the difference between a systematic review and a literature review?

A systematic review is a form of research that uses a rigorous method to generate knowledge from both published and unpublished data. A literature review, on the other hand, is a critical summary of an area of research within the context of what has already been published.

## Table of Contents

- [Stage 2 – Organizing & managing sources](https://www.researchrabbit.ai/articles/best-ai-tools-for-literature-review#stage-2--organizing--managing-sources)
- [Stage 3 – Reading & summarising papers](https://www.researchrabbit.ai/articles/best-ai-tools-for-literature-review#stage-3--reading--summarising-papers)
- [Stage 4 – Analyzing gaps & patterns in literature](https://www.researchrabbit.ai/articles/best-ai-tools-for-literature-review#stage-4--analyzing-gaps--patterns-in-literature)
- [Stage 5 – Writing the literature review section](https://www.researchrabbit.ai/articles/best-ai-tools-for-literature-review#stage-5--writing-the-literature-review-section)
- [Why use AI tools for your literature review?](https://www.researchrabbit.ai/articles/best-ai-tools-for-literature-review#why-use-ai-tools-for-your-literature-review)
- [Tips for using AI tools effectively](https://www.researchrabbit.ai/articles/best-ai-tools-for-literature-review#tips-for-using-ai-tools-effectively)
- [Conclusion](https://www.researchrabbit.ai/articles/best-ai-tools-for-literature-review#conclusion)

A literature review is one of the most time-intensive parts of any research project. Between tracking down relevant papers, keeping them organized, and distilling them into a coherent narrative, it’s easy to feel overwhelmed.

In 2025, AI tools are reshaping this process, helping researchers uncover papers faster, organize their libraries more intelligently, and extract meaningful insights with far less manual effort. Whether you’re preparing for a PhD confirmation, writing a grant proposal, or managing a large-scale research project, the right combination of tools can save you hours and elevate the quality of your work.

In this guide, you’ll find a stage-by-stage breakdown of the best AI tools for literature reviews, along with practical tips on when to use each one and how it can make your process smoother.

## How we chose these tools

This isn’t just a random list pulled from a tech blog. Every tool here has been tried, compared, and selected with the realities of academic research in mind. We focused on four main criteria:

- **Works for real academic research** – These platforms are trusted and used by scholars, not just general productivity apps.

‍
- **Has real AI smarts** – The AI features actually save time or add value — whether by automating repetitive tasks, surfacing relevant papers you’d otherwise miss, or producing clear, accurate summaries.

‍
- **Is easy to get started with** – Clean interfaces and simple onboarding mean you can spend more time researching and less time figuring out how the software works.

‍
- **Covers the whole process** – From finding papers to writing your review, we picked tools that cover every stage of the literature review journey.

## Stage 1 – Finding & collecting research papers

The foundation of any strong literature review is a solid pool of relevant research papers. Instead of relying solely on traditional keyword searches, AI-powered tools now let you map your research area, uncover hidden connections, and find authors you might not have known to search for.

| Tool | What It Does | Why Use It | Limitations | Cost |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ResearchRabbit | Maps research fields, shows related papers & authors, tracks new publications | Ideal for exploring topics visually and staying updated | Smaller coverage than Google Scholar in some fields | Free |
| Litmaps | Visualizes citation networks over time | Great for seeing how ideas evolve chronologically | Works best with well-cited papers | Free basic, Premium plans available |
| Semantic Scholar | AI-powered academic search engine with advanced filters | Relevant results and easy keyword refinement | Some niche topics have fewer indexed papers | Free |
| RDiscovery | AI-powered paper discovery and reading assistant | Good for finding relevant literature quickly and improving reading efficiency | Works best with open-access or institution-access PDFs | Free basic, Premium plans available |

- ‍ **ResearchRabbit**

**‍** Think of it as Spotify for research. Start with one paper and explore related works, key authors, and citation networks visually. It’s perfect for identifying clusters of research and spotting emerging trends in your field. New to it? Check our [Step-by-Step Guide to Using ResearchRabbit for a quick start](https://www.researchrabbit.ai/articles/guide-to-using-researchrabbit).

- **Litmaps**

Helps you see how research in your area has evolved over time. You can follow the story of a topic through citation chains, identify pivotal studies, and spot gaps or emerging areas. The timeline view is especially useful for understanding how ideas develop chronologically.

‍
- **Semantic Scholar** Uses AI to refine searches with advanced filters such as date ranges, author names, and publication types, so you find exactly what you need without sifting through irrelevant results.

‍
- **RDiscovery**

Designed to speed up both discovery and reading, RDiscovery recommends relevant papers based on your topic of interest and integrates AI-assisted reading features like summarization and highlighting. This makes it easier to process large volumes of literature without missing key points.

‍

💡 _Pro tip:_ Start with one key paper in ResearchRabbit and use its “add related works” feature to quickly expand your pool of sources. If you’re not sure where to start, try our [How to Find a Key Research Paper with ResearchRabbit](https://www.researchrabbit.ai/articles/find-a-key-research-paper-with-researchrabbit) guide.

## Stage 2 – Organizing & managing sources

Once you’ve pulled together a stack of promising papers, the real challenge begins: keeping them organized so you can actually use them later. Anyone who’s tried to write a literature review from a messy folder of PDFs knows how quickly important sources can disappear into the shuffle. The right tools, and habits, can save you hours when it’s time to write.

| Tool | What It Does | Why Use It | Limitations | Cost |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Zotero + AI | Organises, tags, and annotates sources | Flexible and open-source | Less advanced visuals | Free / Add-ons |
| EndNote + AI | Citation management with AI suggestions | Great for journal submissions | Paid licence | Paid |
| ResearchRabbit | Discovery + ongoing collection updates | Keeps your library evolving | Not a citation manager | Free |
| Litmaps | Visual evolution of your collection | Tracks connections between your saved papers | Visual focus only | Free / Paid |

- **Zotero** \+ AI Plugins

Store, tag, and annotate your sources. AI plugins can automatically categorize and suggest related materials, making your library smarter over time.

‍
- **EndNote** with AI Add-ons

Ideal for projects with heavy citation needs. EndNote’s integration with AI helps manage complex bibliographies and ensures your references are always in the correct format.

‍
- **ResearchRabbit**

While primarily a discovery tool, it can also help you maintain a living collection by adding newly discovered relevant works to your existing library. This is useful if your topic evolves over time. See [Using ResearchRabbit to Speed Up Literature Review](https://www.researchrabbit.ai/articles/using-researchrabbit-to-speed-up-literature-review) for workflow tips.

‍
- **Litmaps**

Not a traditional reference manager, but perfect for visually tracking how your collection grows and evolves. You can import your saved papers to see how they connect and spot new additions worth including.

‍

💡 _Pro tip:_ Create folders or tags that match your literature review outline in Zotero or EndNote. At the same time, keep a ResearchRabbit project running to catch and integrate any important new papers you might have missed initially.

## Stage 3 – Reading & summarising papers

Once you’ve gathered your papers, the real work begins: actually reading them. And if you’ve got a stack of 40-page PDFs, it’s easy to lose hours (or days) trying to digest every detail. This is where AI can take some of the load off, not by replacing your reading, but by helping you quickly identify what matters.

| Tool | What It Does | Why Use It | Limitations | Cost |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Scholarcy | Summarises PDFs with key details | Saves reading time | May miss subtle context | Free / Paid |
| Elicit | Question-based literature synthesis | Fast targeted answers | Less visual than mapping tools | Free / Paid |
| Anara | AI assistant for summarisation, note-taking, and citation support | Combines summaries with cross-referencing and organised insights | New tool — may require a short learning curve | Free / Paid |

- **Scholarcy**

Upload a PDF, and it generates a structured summary that breaks down the objectives, methods, results, and conclusions. It even extracts key figures and references, so you can immediately see the paper’s core contributions without hunting through the text.

‍
- **Elicit**

Instead of reading every paper front to back, Elicit lets you ask targeted research questions. It then scans your uploaded papers (or its own indexed database) to pull out and summarize relevant findings across multiple studies. This is especially useful when you’re trying to compare how different researchers approached the same problem.

‍
- **Anara**

**‍** Anara acts like an intelligent research assistant; you can upload papers, ask questions about them, and receive clear, contextualized summaries. It’s great for cross-referencing findings, organizing insights, and even generating citations, all in one place. Every answer links back to the exact source locations, which makes verification fast and reliable

💡 _Pro tip:_ Start with Scholarcy for a high-level overview, then use Elicit to dive deeper into pattern-based questions. After that, drop your most important PDFs into Anara to synthesize insights, cross-reference findings, and compile organized reading notes.

## Stage 4 – Analyzing gaps & patterns in literature

A literature review isn’t just about summarising what exists, it’s about showing where the field has been and where it might be heading next. This means actively looking for patterns, contradictions, and unanswered questions. Done manually, this step can be slow and overwhelming. With the right AI tools, you can accelerate the process while still maintaining the depth and nuance of your analysis.

| Tool | What It Does | Why Use It | Limitations | Cost |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ResearchRabbit | Builds visual citation networks showing thematic clusters and connections. | Great for identifying underexplored subfields, overlooked links, and “orphan” studies. | Less effective for topics with very few existing publications. | Free |
| Litmaps | Maps the evolution of research topics through citation chains. | Excellent for spotting turning points, emerging trends, and gaps in literature. | Requires at least one seed paper to generate maps. | Free / Paid |
| Perplexity | Uses AI to answer domain-specific research questions and identify patterns across multiple sources. | Quickly extracts recurring methods, limitations, and gaps without reading every paper in full. | Quality of results depends on prompt clarity and source coverage. | Free / Paid |

- **ResearchRabbit**

While many people use [ResearchRabbit](https://researchrabbitapp.com/) for discovery, it’s equally valuable for analysis. Its visual citation maps make it easier to see isolated subfields, underexplored connections, or orphan studies that don’t fit neatly into existing clusters, which can be a goldmine for gap identification.

‍
- **Litmaps**

Excellent for tracking how a topic has evolved over time. By following citation chains, you can see the “story arc” of an idea and pinpoint moments where research took a new direction. These turning points often reveal opportunities for original contributions.

‍
- **Perplexity**

Acts as an AI research assistant that can analyze large sets of literature to identify trends, recurring methods, and common gaps. You can ask targeted questions (e.g., What methods are most common in this field? or What limitations are frequently mentioned?) and get consolidated answers without reading every paper line-by-line. Precision in your prompts leads to stronger results.

## Stage 5 – Writing the literature review section

This is where your organization and insights pay off, transforming notes into a structured, credible academic narrative. At this stage, clarity and scholarly tone matter just as much as the content itself.

| Tool | What It Does | Why Use It | Limitations | Cost |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ChatGPT | Structure and draft ideas | Flexible and creative | Needs fact-checking | Free / Paid |
| Writefull | Grammar and style check | Optimised for academic writing | Limited creative suggestions | Free / Paid |
| Paperpal | Academic refinement | Journal-level polish | Paid for full features | Free / Paid |

- **ChatGPT** (with academic prompts)

Great for brainstorming structure, creating detailed outlines, and refining complex sentences into something clear and concise.

‍
- **Writefull**

Focused on academic writing quality. It checks grammar, tone, and style against scholarly standards, ensuring consistency across your work.

‍
- **Paperpal**

**‍** Offers instant academic language feedback, suggesting improvements for clarity, style, and flow. It’s particularly good at aligning your writing with journal-level standards, making it a great last step before submission.

💡 Pro tip: Draft with ChatGPT, polish with Writefull, and do a final academic refinement pass with Paperpal to ensure your review is ready for peer review or publication.

‍

## Why use AI tools for your literature review?

Incorporating AI tools into your literature review isn’t just about working faster, it’s about working smarter and producing a review that’s more comprehensive, better organized, and strategically positioned in your field. Having gone through the process myself, I know that the right tools can make the difference between a scattered, overwhelming search and a focused, insightful piece of scholarship.

‍

![why use AI tools for your literature review](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68e48475ee531d1296ee5306/68e4858d612b81f031ca68ba_689bd1e0da13cf19bd17c042_why-use-ai-tools-for-lit-review.webp)

why use AI tools for your literature review?

1. **Speed & efficiency**

AI can scan, filter, and summarize hundreds of papers in minutes. This frees you from the repetitive grind of manual searching and lets you focus on interpretation and synthesis, the parts of the process that actually move your research forward.

‍
2. **Broader coverage**

**‍** When you rely only on your own keywords, you risk missing important but less obvious work. AI-powered discovery tools can surface hidden connections, niche subfields, and influential authors you might not have thought to search for.

‍
3. **Better organization**

**‍** With AI-assisted tagging, categorization, and citation management, your library stays organized from day one. This means fewer headaches when you start writing, and less chance of losing track of an important paper buried in a downloads folder.

‍
4. **Deeper insights**

**‍** AI analysis tools can reveal patterns, contradictions, and recurring gaps across the literature — insights that are much harder to see when you’re buried in PDFs. This helps you position your work where it will have the most impact.

‍
5. **Higher-quality writing**

**‍** From structuring your arguments to refining your academic tone, AI writing assistants can help you present your ideas with more clarity and confidence, making your literature review more persuasive and publication-ready.

## Tips for using AI tools effectively

- **Mix and match tools at every stage**

**‍** No single platform covers everything. Using a combination of discovery, organization, and analysis tools gives you a more complete picture of the literature.

‍
- **Verify AI summaries against the source**

**‍** AI can save you time, but it can also oversimplify. Always skim the original paper to confirm the details and avoid missing critical nuances.

‍
- **Stay updated**

**‍** Save your searches, set alerts, and revisit your projects regularly so you catch new publications as soon as they appear.

‍
- ‍ **Organize as you go**

**‍** Tag, label, and annotate your papers while you’re collecting them. When it’s time to write, you’ll thank yourself for having everything already sorted.

## Conclusion

AI tools are no longer just nice to have in academic research, they’re essential companions for anyone tackling a literature review in 2025. By combining the strengths of different platforms at each stage, you can work faster, stay more organized, and produce a literature review that’s not only thorough but also strategically positioned in your field. Whether you’re mapping a new research area, spotting gaps, or polishing your final draft, the right AI stack makes the process smoother and more rewarding.

If you’re ready to get started, begin by mapping your field with [**ResearchRabbit**](https://researchrabbitapp.com/) to uncover key papers and research clusters. Once you see the landscape clearly, every other stage of your literature review becomes faster, sharper, and more rewarding.

‍

[![ResearchRabbit intuitive exploration and powerful visualizations](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68367775ca3b1655a71e1698/68f19b1c430a475c1db99e26_Untitled%20(300%20x%20300%20px)%20(800%20x%201036%20px).webp)](https://researchrabbitapp.com/)

# Elicit vs. SciSpace: AI research assistant for effortless literature review

Elicit AI vs. SciSpace — A comparison of the two best AI research assistants that streamline your literature review process.

Aug 18, 2023— [Madalsa](https://scispace.com/resources/author/madalsa/ "Madalsa"), [Sumalatha Gangadhar](https://scispace.com/resources/author/sumalatha/ "Sumalatha Gangadhar")

![Elicit vs. SciSpace: AI research assistant for effortless literature review](https://scispace.com/resources/content/images/size/w2400/2023/08/blog-post-07.png)

## Elicit vs. SciSpace: AI research assistant for effortless literature review

**_Quick snapshot:_** _This article entails a high-level comparison of the two best AI tools that helps researchers streamline the literature review process — Elicit AI research assistant and SciSpace literature review._

In academics, the challenges faced by researchers and Ph.D. students are multifaceted.

The task of sifting through vast publications, finding relevant articles, and then comparing them can be overwhelming.

Moreover, to ensure the quality of academic work, it's crucial to deep dive into these articles, extract key information, and obtain specific paper summaries. This rigorous process is termed as a ' [literature review](https://knox.libguides.com/literature-review?ref=scispace.com),' which is notably time-consuming.

Thankfully, AI tools like Elicit and SciSpace are here to simplify this journey!

Research assistants such as Elicit AI and SciSpace are certainly revolutionizing the literature review process, making it more efficient and accurate for users. How?

These AI-powered tools work based on large language models to simplify the research tasks. You can automate research workflows and find relevant articles, extract key information, brainstorm ideas, get paper specific summary, and much more!

Without further ado, let's delve deep into these two AI tools — Elicit AI and SciSpace Literature review and explore their benefits and features in detail.

[SciSpace Literature Review - Get to the bottom of scientific literature\\
\\
SciSpace Literature Review is an interactive literature review workstation where you can find scientific articles, gather meaningful insights, and compare multiple sources. All in one place.\\
\\
![](https://typeset.io/icon.png)Get to the bottom of scientific literature\\
\\
![](https://typeset.io/vs/meta-images/search.png)](https://typeset.io/search?ref=scispace.com) SciSpace Literature review - Interactive research

## What is Elicit (The AI Research Assistant)?

Elicit AI leverages advanced LLMs ( [Large Language Models](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/glossary/data-science/large-language-models/?ref=scispace.com)) to help you navigate and understand relevant research articles in-depth.

It harnesses the power of large language models to automate research workflows, focusing on open-ended reasoning.

By supervising reasoning processes rather than outcomes, it aims to scale literature reviews and streamline research tasks. This compositional approach, developed using the [Interactive Composition Explorer](https://ought.org/updates/2022-10-06-ice-primer?ref=scispace.com#:~:text=useful%20in%20practice.-,Interactive%20Composition%20Explorer%20(ICE),Reuse%20component%20recipes%20such%20as%20question%2Danswering%2C%20ranking%2C%20and%20verification,-ICE%20looks%20like) (ICE), ensures robust and well-reasoned research outcomes.

![Elicit-AI-Research-Assistant](https://scispace.com/resources/content/images/2023/08/Elicit-AI-research-assistant.png)Elicit AI research assistant

AI research assistant Elicit streamlines the process of finding, summarizing, and understanding research articles. You can extract key information, abstract summary, and paper-specific insights from multiple papers on a single screen.

### How to search on Elicit?

All you have to do is upload a PDF or search for a topic or a keyword (regardless of the perfect keyword match).

Elicit AI will curate the top-most relevant papers based on the search terms. However, you can't find relevant articles using the DOI link on Elicit.

This AI tool aims to make the literature review more efficient, accurate, and insightful, reducing the time and effort traditionally required to stay updated with the latest studies and findings in a particular field.

## What is SciSpace Literature Review?

SciSpace employs advanced LLMs (Large Language Models) to simplify researchers' workflows like literature reviews and understanding confused and complex academic texts.

![SciSpace-Literature-Review](https://scispace.com/resources/content/images/2023/08/SciSpace-literature-review.png)SciSpace Literature Review

It also helps you automate research workflows and assists you in finding relevant papers even without perfect keyword match or research questions.

SciSpace highlights the main conclusions, summarize takeaways, TL;DR, methodologies, and more in an easy-to-read table format.

### How to find relevant papers on SciSpace?

All you have to do is enter the keyword or any research question on the search tab and get insights from the top 5 most relevant papers based on your query. Then, you can analyze the results, add, or remove papers and even customize the table based on your research needs.

Similar to Elicit AI, SciSpace ensures that you can efficiently find the most relevant journal articles, accelerating your research process and improving the quality of your literature review and other research tasks.

[Learn more about SciSpace Literature review workspace](https://scispace.com/resources/scispace-literature-review-workspace/).

## Elicit AI Vs. SciSpace literature review — Head-to-Head comparison

Here we'll take a side-by-side look at the SciSpace Literature review and AI research assistant Elicit to shed light on their features.

Let's unveil what each brings to the table to automate research workflows and other research tasks.

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Features | Elicit AI | SciSpace |
| Custom Columns | Add additional information about all papers — Intervention, outcomes, etc. | Add custom columns to extract information such as conclusion, methodologies, etc. |
| Language support | Primarily English-focused | Supports interaction in 75+ languages |
| Paper analysis and summarization | Provides insights from top 4 papers with concise summaries | Provides high-level insights from top five papers, with detailed summaries and insights |
| Literature repository | Searches papers across multiple databases | Access to 270+ million research papers, spanning old to recent publications |
| Customization | Advanced search filters including keywords, publishing date, and study type. | Refines results based publication type, year, and section-specific information extraction |
| AI-research assistant support | Allows interaction with research papers for deeper insights. | Integrated with Copilot AI assistant to chat with your paper by asking following up questions |
| Export and save options | Supports BIB and CSV file export. | Exports results as CSV and saves search settings for future sessions. |
| Ease of access | Sign-up to use | Use without signing-up |

### **Language Support**

For non-English-speaking researchers, while Elicit AI is primarily English-focused, SciSpace helps you identify and interact with relevant papers in 75+ languages, ensuring a better understanding of research papers in a language of your choice!

### **Ease of access**

While Elicit AI requires you to sign up before exploring its features, SciSpace offers a more open approach.

With SciSpace, you can explore the tool without the initial commitment of signing up, providing a seamless introduction to its capabilities and allowing you to experience its benefits.

### **Research paper analysis**

AI research assistant Elicit allows you to search or upload PDFs (full text), then provides insights from the top 4 papers with citations. It also generates concise paper summaries, allowing you to grasp the core idea of the paper.

In contrast, SciSpace provides high-level insights from the top papers backed by citations for every query or PDF. It also provides paper-specific summaries, insights, conclusions, TL;DR, and more for each paper that appears after the search.

The best part? With the integration of SciSpace [ChatPDF](https://scispace.com/chat-pdf?utm_source=scispace-resources&utm_medium=blog&utm_campaign=adobe-pdf-reader) AI, you have a tool adept at answering questions about any specific paper. This not only provides you with a quick overview summary but also empowers you to extract key information, ensuring you always find relevant papers with ease.

### **Literature repository**

Elicit AI locates papers related to specific research topics across multiple databases.  In contrast, SciSpace provides access to a staggering repository of more than 270+ million research papers.

These exhaustive repositories ensure that you have unparalleled access to academic literature, reducing the chances of missing pivotal studies.

### **Customization**

Elicit offers advanced search filters that allow you to narrow down results, ensuring a targeted approach to literature review. You can filter papers by specifying keywords, publishing dates, and study types.

Similarly, SciSpace refines results based on criteria such as journal publication type, year, open access, or PDF.

Both Elicit AI and SciSpace offer the ability to extract key information from specific sections of a paper, ensuring precision in finding highly relevant research papers.

### **Ask in-depth questions about the research papers**

With the integration of SciSpace Copilot AI, you have a tool adept at answering questions about any specific paper using large language models. This not only provides you with a quick overview summary but also empowers you to extract key information, ensuring you always find relevant papers with ease.

The main focus of this AI tool is to transform the research review process into an interactive experience. You can ask follow-up questions to academic journals, get in-depth insights from the papers and understand them better.

[How to use SciSpace Copilot](https://scispace.com/resources/introducing-copilot-ai-assistant-explains-research-papers/), our AI research assistant to chat with your PDF?

Similarly, Elicit AI also allows you to dig deeper, find relevant papers and get more insights with the help of an AI research assistant bot.

### **Export and Save options**

While Elicit supports both BIB file, and CSV file export, SciSpace helps you export search results only as a CSV file. Additionally, researchers can save search settings, ensuring a seamless continuation of literature reviews in future sessions.

## Wrapping up the analysis of Elicit AI vs. SciSpace

Undoubtedly, AI research assistant Elicit and SciSpace have proven to be the [best literature review tools](https://scispace.com/resources/online-tools-for-easy-literature-review/), offering unique features to facilitate the literature review process. These tools excel with their AI-driven paper analysis across advanced bibliography repositories making the research-finding process more efficient.

However, SciSpace stands out with its multi-language support and Copilot interactive AI assistant by answering questions in 75+ languages!

But, wait, the best way to gauge the right fit is to experience them firsthand.

So, why not explore these cutting-edge AI tools and see the difference they can make in your research workflow?

Start with your query now — [SciSpace Literature review](https://scispace.com/search?utm_source=scispace-resources&utm_medium=blog&utm_campaign=scispace-literature-review).

## FAQs

### 1\. What is elicit ai?


Elicit AI assists researchers in efficiently navigating academic literature, offering direct access to relevant studies and insights. Thus, streamlining the literature review process.




### 2\. What tool is useful for literature review?


Both Elicit and SciSpace are useful tools for conducting literature reviews, with each offering unique features to aid researchers.



### 3\. What is similar to elicit AI?

SciSpace is a tool similar to Elicit AI, designed to assist in conducting systematic literature reviews.



### 4\. Which AI tool is best for literature review?


SciSpace is an ultimate research assistant for effortless literature review process.



### 5\. How to use Elicit for literature review?


To use Elicit for literature review, You have to sign up. After, you can input your research query, and the tool would then locate research papers and provide insights from relevant academic papers.



### 6\. What is the best AI for academic research?


Elicit and SciSpace are among the top AI tools for academic research, each offering features such as finding relevant research papers and understanding them. Each tool is tailored to streamline your research process.

### See where the research agrees

Ask a clear yes-or-no research question and the Consensus Meter instantly shows how much the evidence agrees or disagrees — giving you a fast, visual read on the state of the research.

Is vitamin D supplementation effective at preventing fractures?

Are antidepressants effective for mild depression?

Is screen time associated with reduced attention in children?
